spec.goal: Implement the first runtime slice of post-In-Review autonomy by letting Maestro classify an owned PR-backed lane as wait_for_review, needs_review_repair, ready_to_land, or blocked from authoritative GitHub review and check state.
success_criteria: Maestro can re-read the recorded PR for an owned In Review lane and classify the lane as wait_for_review, needs_review_repair, ready_to_land, or blocked without mutating the lane.
The classification uses authoritative PR identity, review-thread state, review verdicts, required-check state, and mergeability instead of user chat relay.
The current runtime exposes the classification through status or deterministic logs for the owned lane and handles the absence of an eligible In Review lane explicitly.
Docs and targeted tests cover the classification rules and the ownership boundary between signal intake and later repair or landing work.
constraints: Implement against the accepted XY-160 and XY-176 lifecycle and owned-lane policy instead of inventing new skill-specific states.
Do not perform repair, merge, closeout, or cleanup in this issue.
Do not rely on Linear comment rereads alone for PR identity because the current tracker abstraction does not expose comment or attachment history.
Keep the branch rooted at the latest origin/main in the isolated XY-173 workspace; do not touch the root checkout's local WORKFLOW.md modification.

defaults.status_surface: reuse local status/reporting surfaces before adding new commands
defaults.authoritative_pr_identity: must be persisted by the runtime, not reconstructed from chat

tasks.title: Pin the persisted review-handoff identity and signal model
tasks.objective: Trace where an owned In Review lane can recover its authoritative PR identity today, extend persisted runtime state where needed, and codify the exact signal inputs XY-173 will classify.
outputs: Persisted runtime representation for the owned review handoff PR identity and lineage
Documented classifier input set bound to existing retained-lane state
verification: Targeted unit tests for persisted review-handoff identity or recovery metadata

tasks.title: Implement the review-state classifier and operator-visible surface
tasks.objective: Add the GitHub review/check classifier for owned In Review lanes, wire it into the runtime's local status/reporting surface, and make the no-lane path deterministic.
outputs: Classifier implementation returning wait_for_review, needs_review_repair, ready_to_land, or blocked
Status/log output exposing the classification for the owned lane
Tests covering wait, repair, ready_to_land, blocked, and no-lane cases

tasks.title: Align docs and finish verification for the slice
tasks.objective: Update specs and plan evidence so the accepted runtime surface, operator expectations, and tests match the implemented classifier slice.
outputs: Spec and doc updates for the review-state classifier slice
Plan evidence recording the implementation and verification outcomes

triggers: The retained-lane state cannot safely persist PR identity without expanding the tracker abstraction or state model beyond the scoped classifier slice.
The smallest safe operator-visible surface is not the existing status/log path and would require a new command or wire protocol.
Authoritative GitHub review or check signals differ materially from the XY-160 lifecycle assumptions.

evidence: Added `src/github.rs` for repo-local GitHub identity routing plus PR URL parsing, and persisted `.maestro-review-handoff` markers in `src/state.rs` and `src/agent/tracker_tool_bridge.rs` so retained review lanes can rebind to authoritative PR identity without relying on tracker comment rereads.

Implemented post-review lane classification in `src/orchestrator.rs` using GitHub GraphQL review/check state and exposed it through the existing operator `status` surface as `wait_for_review`, `needs_review_repair`, `ready_to_land`, or `blocked`.

Added regression coverage for review handoff marker roundtrip, review handoff marker persistence, ready-to-land status, missing-marker blocked status, unresolved-thread repair classification, and repo-local GitHub identity routing.

Updated `docs/spec/system_maestro_runtime.md` and `docs/spec/system_maestro_post_review_lifecycle.md` to document the `.maestro-review-handoff` marker and the read-only post-review status surface introduced by XY-173.
Verification passed on the XY-173 lane: `cargo make fmt-check`, `cargo make lint`, `cargo make test`, and `git diff --check`.

Pre-commit self-review tightened the status surface so a GitHub PR state read failure degrades the affected post-review lane to `blocked` with reason `pull_request_state_read_failed` instead of aborting the entire `status` command.

Review repair propagated `codex.github-identity` and `codex.linear-workspace` into clone-backed workspaces so lane-local GitHub and Linear routing survives `issue_review_handoff` and post-review status inspection without manual workspace config.

Review repair paginated GitHub review-thread inspection in `src/orchestrator.rs`, counted unresolved threads beyond the first page, and added guards plus tests for mixed-snapshot pagination and missing end-cursor responses.

key_findings: The tracker abstraction currently exposes issue fields, labels, blockers, and write-side comments, but not comment or attachment rereads.
The accepted XY-160 lifecycle already defines the classifier outcomes and authoritative signals for post-In-Review lanes.
The new XY-173 lane had to be rebased to origin/main because the root checkout is intentionally behind and carries a local WORKFLOW.md modification.
